pr.title: Decompile a handful of functions in mplib.c
pr.author: eperdew

summary: Decompiled several mpLib functions in src/melee/mp/mplib.c, replacing placeholder comments for mpLib_800588D0, mpLib_80058970, mpLib_800589D0, mpLib_80058AA0, and mpLib_8005A2DC with matching C. The PR also added static .sdata globals, introduced mp_UnkStruct7 in mp/types.h and forward.h, updated mplib.h prototypes for newly understood float arguments/returns, and added needed baselib and forward includes. The author reported 100% matches for the listed functions and .sdata matching; review feedback was positive but minimal.
what_changed: Implemented mpLib_800588D0 as a linked-list traversal over mp_UnkStruct7 nodes rooted at static mpLib_804D64C4, updating bit flags based on rectangle overlap tests and setting mpLib_804D64B0 to 1.
Implemented mpLib_80058970 as a two-point bounding rectangle normalizer that computes left/right and bottom/top from four float arguments before calling mpLib_800588D0.
Implemented mpLib_800589D0 as a four-point bounding box builder from eight float arguments before calling mpLib_800588D0.
Implemented mpLib_80058AA0 as a linked-list traversal clearing flag bit 0x1000 on every mp_UnkStruct7 node and resetting mpLib_804D64B0 to 0.
Implemented mpLib_8005A2DC with calls to mpLib_800590F4, mpLib_80059E60, OSReport, and HSD_StateInvalidate; it records a one-time report guarded by mpLib_804D64D0.
Added static globals mpLib_804D64C4 through mpLib_804D64E4 in mplib.c, including a pointer to mp_UnkStruct7 and several s32 counters/values, supporting the author's note that .sdata was matched.
Added struct mp_UnkStruct7 to src/melee/mp/types.h with next pointer, flag fields, padding, and four float bounds at offsets 0x10, 0x14, 0x18, and 0x1C.
Added a forward declaration for mp_UnkStruct7 in src/melee/mp/forward.h.
Updated mplib.h prototypes for mpLib_80058970 and mpLib_800589D0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void functions with f32 arguments, and changed mpLib_80059E60 to return float.
smart_moves: Kept newly inferred layout minimal by naming the new type mp_UnkStruct7 and using offset-style field names rather than overclaiming semantic names.
Matched branch-sensitive rectangle normalization logic by preserving explicit left/right and bottom/top comparisons instead of replacing with library min/max helpers that might alter codegen.

Separated the shared rectangle invalidation work into mpLib_800588D0 and expressed mpLib_80058970/mpLib_800589D0 as wrappers that compute bounds then delegate, matching the apparent original call structure.
Used exact flag masks such as 0x10000, 0x40000, 0x400, 0x1000, and 0xFFFFEFFF rather than introducing speculative enum names.
Preserved the apparent typo in the OSReport format string for mpLib_8005A2DC while leaving a comment questioning it, which avoids changing data/string matching while documenting the anomaly.
decomp_lessons: For early mplib decomp work, matching may require declaring nearby .sdata globals in address order even before their full semantics are known.
When a function operates on a linked list of unknown records, a minimal offset-accurate struct can unlock multiple matching functions without needing final semantic field names.
Header prototypes should be tightened as soon as argument and return types are known; this PR converted unknown mplib prototypes to f32/float signatures where the decompiled code required them.
Do not silently correct typos in embedded format strings if the goal is matching; preserve the binary string and document uncertainty in comments.
Bounds-building helpers often need source-like comparison structure for matching, especially when computing min/max over float arguments.
naming_conventions: Unknown map-library structs use the mp_UnkStructN pattern, e.g. mp_UnkStruct7.
Unknown struct fields retain offset-style names such as x4, x8, x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
Static data symbols follow the mpLib_804D64XX address-based naming convention.
Function names retain the mpLib_800XXXXX address-based convention until semantic names are known.
Float arguments in updated prototypes were named arg8 through argF in mplib.h, reflecting uncertainty rather than semantic labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit if/else comparisons to compute bounding extents instead of abstract min/max calls, likely to preserve exact control flow and floating-point codegen.
Maintained exact bitwise operations on x8 flags, including clearing bit 0x1000 with mask 0xFFFFEFFF.
Added includes for mp/forward.h, baselib/tev.h, and baselib/texp.h to satisfy newly compiled references and type dependencies.
Introduced mpLib_804D64C4 as a typed static pointer so linked-list traversal could compile cleanly and match.
Kept mpLib_8005A2DC's OSReport string byte-for-byte despite suspected format-string typos, preserving data matching.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Positive acceptance feedback: "This is great, thank you. No need to talk on Discord if you prefer GitHub."
review_feedback.action_taken: No concrete code changes from review feedback are evidenced in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or refine mp_UnkStruct7 fields x8, x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
Search for later changes to the OSReport string in mpLib_8005A2DC to see whether the suspected typos were ever addressed.
Look for subsequent mplib.c PRs involving mpLib_804D64C4 or the 0x1000 invalidation flag.
Search callers of mpLib_80058970 and mpLib_800589D0 to infer semantic names for their float coordinate arguments.
Check later work on mpLib_80058ACC, mpLib_8005A220, and mpLib_8005A340, which remained undecompiled in this slice.
